worthClicking.author: @bcherny
worthClicking.why: First-party Anthropic signal on what Opus 4.7 actually changes in Claude Code — more agentic, cross-session context, better ambiguity handling.

worthClicking.author: @FakePsyho
worthClicking.why: Sharp one-liner with image showing benchmark inflation has entered absurdist territory — directly relevant to evaluating model claims.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Detailed trust-regression critique of Opus 4.7 vs 4.6 — the 'disgruntled employee' framing captures the reliability problem product teams are actually hitting.

worthClicking.author: @theo
worthClicking.why: Theo's IE analogy for Claude Code complacency is the kind of pushback that shapes product team standards — worth knowing this take is circulating.

worthClicking.author: @jpschroeder
worthClicking.why: Concise articulation of the Mythos narrative gap: if the god-model exists, why is the current product stack so broken right now.

worthClicking.author: @zachtratar
worthClicking.why: Raises a real governance question: Anthropic's CPO just left Figma's board and they immediately shipped a Figma competitor — conflict of interest angle worth tracking.

worthClicking.author: @zebriez
worthClicking.why: Case study on Notion as a rare pre-AI company that actually made the leap to AI-native — useful reference for product strategy conversations.

worthClicking.author: @gregisenberg
worthClicking.why: Identifies 'AI that pushes back' as an underexplored product category — directly relevant to AI product strategy and trust design.

worthClicking.author: @thsottiaux
worthClicking.why: Inside look at Claude Mac computer use from someone close to the build — background operation without disrupting flow is the key product detail.

worthClicking.author: @PeterDiamandis
worthClicking.why: Blunt thesis that LLM companies will systematically disrupt every vertical SaaS to fund compute — the Claude Design move as evidence of the broader pattern.

voices.why: Anthropic-adjacent, posting substantive Claude Code product details not available elsewhere.
Detailed, experience-driven model critiques — the kind of practitioner feedback that matters for AI product reliability decisions.
Thoughtful observation on how AI shifts time allocation in workflows — relevant to product strategy and distribution thinking.
Doing real case study work on AI-native transformations of established companies, not just hot takes.
Consistently identifies product opportunity gaps in AI behavior (sycophancy, branding) that map to real distribution and strategy angles.

examples: viral lifestyle/emotional content (ages you suffer, boring habits, after-work memes)
geopolitical breaking news dumps (Hormuz, Lebanon, Iran) with no AI relevance
crypto shilling and trading advice posts
